semantic.role: Configuration validation layer in the infrastructure module, responsible for validating `SpineConfig` structures against the protocol schema before they are consumed by the rest of the system.
responsibilities: Validate an arbitrary value against the `SpineConfig` protocol schema, enumerating all validation issues.
Provide a typed result interface (`SpineConfigValidationResult`) for consumers to check success/failure.
Expose both a semantic resolver (`resolveSpineConfig`) and a convenience validator (`validateSpineConfig`).
outOfScope: Loading configuration from filesystem, environment variables, or credentials.
Merging config defaults or applying overrides.
Runtime integration of the validated config.
invariants: Must not depend on CLI entry code (complies with core-pipeline-isolation rule).
Returned `SpineConfig` must strictly conform to the protocol version constants.

changeIntent.architecturalIntent: Maintain a clean validation boundary for Spine configuration, ensuring that any config payload is type-checked before entering the core pipeline.
changeIntent.recentChangeIntent: Incorporated release gate fixes and synced accumulated source changes; the validation logic itself remains contract-stable.

publicSurface.description: Takes an unknown value and returns a `SpineConfigValidationResult` containing the parsed config (or null) and a list of validation issues. This is the primary entry point for config validation.
Convenience function that delegates to `resolveSpineConfig` and returns the same result. Intended for simple validation calls where the semantic distinction is not needed.
Interface defining the shape of the validation outcome: `config` (nullable SpineConfig) and `issues` (string array of validation errors/warnings).
